text:
Simone Biles suffered what appeared to be a minor injury scare during her triumphant return toOlympicsaction inParis.

The American turned in superb displays in the artistic gymnastics qualification events on Sunday morning, topping the leader boards to markher returnin style.

She completed all four routines to get Team USA off to an excellent start but there was a brief moment of concern for the 27-year-old following her first event.

Biles briefly exited the competition floor inside Bercy Arena with what appeared to be an ankle injury. She appeared to notice the injury after a warm-up routine ahead of her floor event.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for latest news on the Paris Olympics

‘As soon as I took off, I felt it,’ Biles was heard saying to Team USA doctor Dr Marcia Faustin, ‘It’s right there on my calf. Right where I had that tear.’

Biles still scored a huge 14.600 in the floor event but was seen grimacing on some of her landings during her routine.

She re-emerged for her first vault event wearing heavy strapping on her left foot.

But it did not prevent her from making more Olympic history moments later, becoming the first woman to land the Yurchenko double pike in an Olympic event, scoring a massive 15.800.

Biles finished her morning on the bars, smiling and saluting the crowd as she left the floor.

It was a dazzling display from one of the greatest athletes in Olympic history, whose Tokyo 2020 Gameswas derailed by a bout of the ‘twisties’.

Biles was forced to withdraw from several events and ultimately ended up taking a two-year hiatus from the sport she once dominated.

But her impressive return on Day Two of action in the French capital suggests she is back to her best.

title: Simone Biles suffers injury scare during Olympics return in Paris | Metro News
description: US gymnastics star Simone Biles suffered a major injury scare as she began her 2024 Olympics campaign in Paris on Sunday morning.
